rationale: Local Memory preflight emits trust-boundary readiness evidence before downstream validation runs.
Policy-gate decisions classify file risk and block unsafe work based on machine-readable evidence.
Runtime-card output is operator-facing evidence for local execution and handoff state.
Browser-evidence validation decides whether captured screenshots and console telemetry are sufficient routine UI proof.
Delivery-truth composition decides whether evidence can support closeout claims.
Freshness policy prevents stale or conflicting evidence from becoming claim support.
External-state snapshots separate PR, CI, review, tracker, and merge-readiness truth.
PR closeout reports aggregate delivery evidence and must not infer missing claim support.
